items.title: Elderly woman dies after van crashes into tree
items.summary: JERTIH: An elderly woman was killed after the van she was travelling in skidded and crashed into a tree in Kampung Anak Ikan here about 1.50am today. Besut police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahman said Munah Awang, 76, died while being treated at the Besut Hospital emergency unit about 4.30am. Several other passengers who sustained minor injuries were also sent to Besut Hospital for treatment, he said.

items.title: Negri Sembilan state assembly dissolution looms as MB crisis deepens, analysts warn
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The political crisis gripping Negri Sembilan could end in the dissolution of the state assembly if ongoing negotiations between Umno and Pakatan Harapan on the matter fail to produce a resolution. Universiti Malaya political analyst Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said if talks broke down, the dissolution of the state assembly was the most likely outcome. Yesterday, Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi had said the political standoff in Negri Sembilan is "almost over" .

items.title: Umno adopts out-of-the-box methods to win over young voters, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno will adopt "out-of-the-box" approaches, including non-verbal communication methods, to win over young voters who now form the majority in the country's political landscape. Its president, Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, said this reality must be acknowledged as voters below 40, including those under the Undi18 initiative, are more open-minded and no longer bound by party loyalty alone. Zahid said the strategy is crucial as voting trends have shifted, with voters increasingly assessing candidates based on service delivery and personal credibility rather than the political party they represent.

items.title: KLIA Terminal 2 balcony cordoned off after two foreign deaths
items.summary: SEPANG: The balcony area on Level 3 at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 2, where two foreigners fell to their deaths, has been cordoned off from public access. Police are investigating the two separate deaths that occurred on May 1. When the New Straits Times visited the Green Zone on Level 3, barriers had been erected around the section where the fall occurred, restricting public access.

items.title: Sarawak Water warns of low water pressure in Sibu after cable theft forced electricity shutdown at treatment plant
items.summary: SIBU, May 3 — Sarawak Water Sdn Bhd (Central Region) has warned of possible low water pressure in several areas here following the theft of an earthing cable at the Salim Phase 1 Water Treatment Plant substation. “This may result in low water pressure in certain areas, particularly during the shutdown period,” the utility company said. Sarawak Water said supply is expected to recover gradually once electricity is fully restored and plant operations return to normal.

items.title: Man killed, four injured in two‑vehicle collision near Miri Hospital
items.summary: MIRI, May 3 — A 43-year-old man was killed in a two-vehicle collision early today at a traffic light junction at Jalan Miri Bypass, near Miri Hospital. Miri district police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the accident occurred when a four‑wheel‑drive (4WD) vehicle, driven by a man in his mid‑twenties, lost control while travelling from Permyjaya towards Taman Tunku and veered into the opposite lane. The driver and two other passengers were sent to Miri Hospital for treatment.

items.title: Scams in Singapore: 20 Malaysians arrested in city-state since March
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Twenty Malaysians have been arrested in Singapore since March for allegedly facilitating scam syndicates by collecting cash, gold, and valuables from victims. In the latest case on April 29, a 21-year-old Malaysian woman was arrested for her suspected role as a money mule in an investment scam. Police investigations revealed that a man was lured via a WhatsApp investment group and initially transferred SG$10,000.

items.title: Police refute claim university student stabbed 105 times
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Police today dismissed claims suggesting that a university student found murdered at a village in Ketereh on Friday succumbed to 105 stab wounds. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri said investigations showed the 19-year-old victim died from 61 stab wounds. Azmir said the results of the post-mortem did not include scratches and minor wounds.

items.title: Brace for evening thunderstorms: MetMalaysia warns of heavy downpours across the country
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a severe weather alert, warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds across 13 states until 7.00pm today. Issued at 4.30pm, the short-term advisory cautions residents across the peninsula, as well as parts of Sabah and Sarawak, to brace for imminent heavy downpours. In Kedah, affected areas include Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu.

items.title: Umno to try new playbook for the under-40 crowd
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Umno will adopt ‘out-of-the-box’ approaches, including non-verbal communication methods, to win over young voters who now form the majority in the country’s political landscape. Its president, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, said this reality must be acknowledged, as voters below 40, including those under the Undi18 initiative, are more open-minded and no longer bound by party loyalty alone. Ahmad Zahid said the strategy is crucial as voting trends have shifted, with voters increasingly assessing candidates based on service delivery and personal credibility rather than the political party they represent.

items.title: Lee Lam Thye urges urgent action after Cheras child drowning
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye called for urgent measures, including installation of secure covers on drains, following the death of a nine-year-old girl in Cheras. Lee said drowning remains one of the leading causes of death among children in Malaysia, with hundreds of lives lost each year, while urban drains and flood channels have increasingly become a hidden danger in residential areas. Among the measures proposed were nationwide safety audits of drains, particularly in residential areas, schools and playgrounds, as well as the installation of secure covers or grates over open drains.

items.title: Zahid denies Tok Mat is mastermind of Negri Sembilan leadership crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan plays a key role in easing the Negri Sembilan leadership crisis, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said Mohamad, commonly known as Tok Mat, has consistently sought solutions through discussions with party leaders to defuse the situation. As such, he said allegations linking Mohamad, who is also Rembau member of Parliament, as the mastermind behind the crisis in Negri Sembilan are unfair.

items.title: Sri Lanka raises fuel prices as inflation spikes
items.summary: The island has also rationed fuel following supply disruptions. Petrol rose 12 rupees to 410 rupees (US$1.32). Diesel was up 10 rupees to 392 rupees.

items.title: Ahmad Zahid: ‘Unfair’ to accuse Tok Mat of masterminding Negeri Sembilan crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan plays a key role in easing the current administrative crisis in the Negeri Sembilan government, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said Mohamad, better known as Tok Mat, has consistently sought solutions through discussions with party leaders to defuse the situation. As such, he said allegations linking Mohamad, who is also the member of parliament for Rembau, as the mastermind behind the crisis in Negeri Sembilan are unfair.

items.title: Optimax set for earnings lift on MyAngkasa partnership, says CGS International
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Optimax Holdings Bhd is poised to see a meaningful uplift in earnings following its partnership with MyAngkasa Holdings Sdn Bhd, according to CGS International Securities Malaysia Sdn Bhd. CGS International has raised its core earnings per share (EPS) forecasts for FY26 and FY27 by 6.8 per cent and 3.6 per cent, respectively, factoring in contributions from MyAngkasa patients, the ramp-up of existing ambulatory care centres (ACCs) and the rollout of ear, nose and throat (ENT) services expected to begin in the second quarter of 2026. The firm also introduced its FY28 forecasts, projecting a net profit compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 16.5 per cent over FY25–FY28.

items.title: Two-thirds of proposed amendments to Mara Act focus on strengthening governance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two-thirds of the proposed amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act focus on strengthening governance and improving administrative efficiency within the organisation. Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the act, which has been in force since 1966 with only minor amendments, is no longer aligned with current governance needs and requires comprehensive reform. He said the act centralises significant authority in the chairman and the Mara Council, a structure he said reflects outdated governance practices.

items.title: PublicInvest maintains an "Outperform" call on Avaland, keeping a 30 sen target price
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) has maintained its earnings forecasts for Avaland Bhd while reiterating its "Outperform" call on the property company with a target price of 30 sen, based on a roughly 60 per cent The research house said Avaland's recent purchase of land in Taman U-Thant, Kuala Lumpur, is expected to bolster its position in the luxury residential market. Avaland recently entered into a conditional sale and purchase agreement to acquire three adjoining parcels of freehold land measuring 0.76 hectares (ha) in Taman U-Thant, Kuala Lumpur, for RM86 million. The land, acquired from Tong Ah Company Sdn Bhd, is priced at about RM1,050 per square foot and is earmarked for a high-rise residential development with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM700 million.

items.title: Heavy rain, thunderstorms forecast in KL, Putrajaya and 11 states until 5pm today
items.summary: In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the affected states include Perlis, Penang, and Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Yan, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu). Similar weather conditions are expected in Perak (Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang, and Muallim). Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun, and Kemaman) and Kelantan (Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Kota Bharu, Tanah Merah, Bachok, Machang, Pasir Puteh, and Kuala Krai) are also affected.

items.title: NCMP to boost solar adoption, green infrastructure investment: HLIB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The National Carbon Market Policy (NCMP), which prioritises lower-cost abatement measures for domestic emissions reduction, is expected to accelerate momentum in solar energy adoption and energy efficiency retrofitting, according to Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB). "We understand the recent strength in renewable energy names has been driven by several supportive factors, including more stable solar module prices (US$0.11 to US$0.12 per watt), rising national focus on energy security, and the continued rollout of new renewable energy capacity schemes. HLIB said this is expected to benefit renewable energy players that can supply green electricity bundled with renewable energy certificates, supporting a strong long-term earnings outlook for the sector.

items.title: Nestlé Malaysia outlook steady as cost easing offsets geopolitical risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nestlé (M) Bhd's near-term earnings outlook remains steady despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, with analysts noting that operational resilience and easing input costs are helping to cushion potential risks. In recent briefings, CGS International Securities Malaysia Sdn Bhd said the company's management remains confident that its diversified supply chain, easing cocoa and coffee prices and contingency measures will limit the impact of the conflict on earnings. According to management, Nestlé's geographically diverse supplier base is expected to reduce exposure to supply-side disruptions.

items.title: Uni student stabbed 61 times was kind-hearted, cheerful
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A university student who was found murdered with 61 stab wounds in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, on Friday has been described as a kind-hearted and cheerful person. A friend of the victim, who wished to be known only as Nurul, said that a day before the incident, they had gone out for breakfast with several other friends at a supermarket near their college in Lembah Sireh. "Isya (Nurfisya Zulkifly) was her usual cheerful self.

items.title: Tasco FY26 earnings miss expectations on weaker cold supply chain segment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tasco Bhd reported financial year 2026 (FY26) core earnings that came in below expectations from both RHB Research and market consensus, largely weighed down by weaker performance in its cold supply chain (CSC) segment. RHB Research said Tasco's FY26 core earnings were flattish year on year (YoY) at RM39.8 million, representing 90 per cent of the firm's and 102 per cent of consensus full-year forecasts. Looking ahead, RHB Research said Tasco should deliver positive earnings growth in FY27, driven by the commencement of its two new warehouses by mid-2026.

items.title: Sombre mood as Nur Qaseh's family gathers after her body is found [WATCH]
items.summary: KAJANG: A sombre atmosphere filled the Kajang Hospital forensic unit today as family members of Nur Qaseh Deandra Mohd Qayyum,9, gathered following the recovery of her body from Sungai Langat. Health Ministry counsellors were also present to provide psychosocial support to the grieving family. The girl's grandfather, Zulkarnain Ahmad, 57, told reporters the family was relieved the search had ended, despite the tragic outcome.

items.title: Nearly 90 per cent of Kedah padi farmers yet to begin planting
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Almost 90 per cent of rice farmers in Kedah, including those within the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) areas, have yet to begin planting for Season 1/2026 due to rising operational costs and a shortage in water supply. "The primary issue is the low dam water levels. Rashid said machinery costs have skyrocketed, with tractor rental rates increasing by up to 66 per cent, while harvester costs have risen by about 38 per cent.

items.title: Unity government council to discuss Negeri Sembilan MB row next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The Unity Government Leadership Consultative Council is scheduled to meet next week to discuss a range of current issues, including the status of the Negeri Sembilan menteri besar post. Its secretariat chief, Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, said the meeting is important to deliberate on policy matters and positions among parties in the government, as well as issues involving the monarchy and customs in the state. On Tuesday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun would continue to carry out his duties as menteri besar as consented to by the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.

items.title: Two Gaza flotilla activists to appear before Israeli court
items.summary: ASHKELON, Israel: Two foreign activists from a Gaza-bound flotilla who were brought to Israel for interrogation will appear in court on Sunday, an Israeli rights group said. They were intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters off Greece early on Thursday, with Israel saying it had removed some 175 activists – two of whom were taken to Israel for questioning. On Saturday, Adalah said its lawyers had met the two detained activists at Shikma Prison in Ashkelon.

items.title: Israel issues new evacuation warnings in southern Lebanon beyond occupied area
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel's military on Sunday issued new evacuation warnings in southern Lebanon for villages beyond the area it occupies, despite a truce with Lebanon intended to halt fighting with Iran-backed Hizbollah. Lebanon's state-run National News Agency later reported a series of Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon, including on towns not mentioned in the evacuation warning. On Wednesday Israel's military chief of staff Eyal Zamir had threatened to strike Hizbollah "beyond the yellow line", which marks the area of Israeli control.

items.title: Mara vows safety overhaul after killing of 19-year-old student
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has instructed Kolej Poly-Tech Mara Sdn Bhd (KPTMSB) to carry out a comprehensive review of student safety and welfare measures to prevent any recurrence of violent incidents, including murder, involving its students. Asyraf said any shortcomings in safety and welfare must be examined in detail and improved to ensure student well-being. "I have asked the management of Kolej Poly-Tech Mara to review student safety and welfare comprehensively.

items.title: Asyraf Wajdi: Umno, PH to meet next week over Negri Sembilan crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno and Pakatan Harapan will address ongoing political tensions in Negri Sembilan through the unity government leadership consultative council meeting next week. Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the situation in the state is related to party policies and the respective positions of both parties. Asyraf Wajdi said one of Umno's political bureau resolutions recently was for both sides to engage and resolve matters relating to policy and party positions.

items.title: Fadhlina orders review of SPM Moral Studies after furore over ‘unfair’ grading
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek has ordered a full review of the SPM Moral Studies subject, stepping in after an outcry from parents and top-performing students whose university prospects are being jeopardised by unexpectedly poor grades in the paper. Speaking at an event in Puchong today, Fadhlina acknowledged the “anxieties of parents and the community” and directed the Malaysian Examinations Syndicate to conduct the review. The review comes after reports that many high-achieving students received unexpectedly poor grades for the subject, potentially jeopardising their eligibility for matriculation programmes which often have stringent grade requirements.

items.title: Iran plans to turn university site hit in US-Israeli strikes into museum
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iranian authorities plan to turn a bombsite at a university in central Iran into a museum about the impact of US-Israeli strikes, an official said. "The current damaged site will be preserved as a war museum at the university to remain a document of the country's scientific oppression in history," said Zafarollah Kalantari, head of Isfahan University of Technology on Saturday. Kalanari said initial assessments had put the cost of the damage to the university's buildings and facilities at around US$11 million.

items.title: 84-year-old man feared drowned in Sungai Petani river
items.summary: A fire and rescue team is currently conducting a search and rescue (SAR) operation at the scene. Tikam Batu Fire and Rescue Station chief Assistant Superintendent Fauzi Shuaib said his team received a call at 8.56am. "The search operation is led by Zone 2 Officer of the Kedah Fire and Rescue Department, Fire Superintendent Bryan Del Bungkias, together with eight personnel from the Tikam Batu Fire and Rescue Station.

items.title: Queen departs for Uzbekistan to attend international Islamic civilization conference
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, has departed for Tashkent, Uzbekistan today to attend an international conference on Islamic civilisation. The Queen will participate in the conference titled "Scientific Tradition in Islamic Civilisation: Sources, Potentials and Challenges", scheduled to take place at the Center of Islamic Civilisation in Tashkent. Earlier today, Her Majesty departed for Uzbekistan from the Royal Hangar at Senai International Airport.

items.title: Malaysians warned over dubious 'badal haj' offers
items.summary: JEDDAH: Malaysians are advised not to be fooled by dubious offers of badal haj (haj by proxy) services, especially those offered at "extraordinarily" low prices compared to the actual cost of the pilgrimage. Malaysian Consul General in Jeddah Tengku Mohd Dzaraif Raja Abdul Kadir said the cost of performing haj is high, hence any unreasonable offer is feared to not meet the prescribed pillars and obligatory haj. Mohd Dzaraif explained that for domestic haj packages in Saudi Arabia itself, the minimum cost is around RM9,000 to RM10,000.

items.title: MCMC warns against sharing provocative 3R content online
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission has issued a stern warning to the public against producing or distributing online content touching on race, religion, and the royal institution (3R), particularly material that is provocative, insulting, incites hatred, or risks triggering public tension. "The MCMC takes seriously the misuse of network services and online platforms that could harm social harmony. The commission said it would work with the police and relevant agencies to monitor, investigate, and take action against content that violated the law.

items.title: MCMC, police to act on provocative 3R material
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The public has been reminded not to produce or disseminate content related to race, religion and royalty (3R), particularly material that is provocative, insulting, incites hatred or could trigger public tension. In a statement today, the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) said it takes seriously the misuse of network services and online platforms that could undermine social harmony. MCMC also said it will work closely with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and relevant agencies to monitor, investigate and take action against content that violates the law.

items.title: Health Ministry says 56 heat-related illnesses, two deaths reported since January
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 3 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has recorded 56 cases related to hot weather nationwide, including two deaths from heat stroke, from Jan 1 to today. According to the statement, there were 47 cases involved heat exhaustion, four cases of exertional heat stroke, four cases of heat stroke and one case of heat cramps. MOH said analysis showed that 58 per cent of cases of heat-related illnesses were related to physical activity during hot weather.

items.title: Man dies 2 days after Johor crash, buried beside wife and daughter
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: A Proton Persona driver involved in a crash at Km71, Jalan Johor Baru–Mersing on Friday died last night. Mohd Aiman, 20, the third child of Kamilin Alias, 56, and Rubeta Mohd, 51, confirmed the death when contacted yesterday. He said his father (Kamilin) was pronounced dead at 10.35pm.

items.title: Missing Bandar Mahkota Cheras girl found dead in Sungai Langat after three-day search
items.summary: KAJANG, May 3 — The body of a nine-year-old girl who fell into a drain and was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras two days ago was found in Sungai Langat near Jalan Reko here today. Kajang Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief and operations commander Izman Adnan said Nur Qaseh Deandra Mohd Qayyum’s remains were discovered about 10.6 kilometres from where she was reported to have fallen. Izman said rescue teams faced challenges accessing entry points to Sungai Long, but favourable weather conditions throughout the operation helped facilitate the movement of personnel.

items.title: Mental health at work to become legal duty for employers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) is moving to make mental health protection a statutory requirement for employers, framing psychosocial wellbeing as a legal duty under the Occupational Safety and Health Act. The push to strengthen mental health protection forms part of the Occupational Safety and Health Master Plan 2026–2030 (OSHMP30), which the department began implementing earlier this year. Strategic Thrust 2 of the plan focuses on enhancing worker health and wellbeing, including mental health promotion, occupational health management, and the creation of workplaces that are safe from both physical and psychosocial hazards.

items.title: Anwar orders Mara Act amendments to be completed within three months
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed that the draft amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act be completed within three months. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the move includes streamlining Mara’s role as a key umbrella for the Bumiputera economy as well as a one-stop centre for development through better coordination of related agencies and policies. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki and Mara director-general Datuk Zulfikri Osman.

items.title: North to south, west to east: MetMalaysia warns of stormy weather nationwide, including Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to affect the capital and eight other states until 3 pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). The department also forecast similar weather conditions across Kuala Lumpur and parts of Selangor, namely Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang. Similar conditions are also expected in Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah and Rembau) and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru), as well as in Sabah involving Tawau (Lahad Datu), Sandakan (Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan) and Kudat (Pitas and Kudat).

items.title: Early morning blaze guts five cars at George Town flat
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said his department received a call about the fire at 4.30 am and a fire brigade team from the Jalan Perak Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) immediately went to the location. “Upon arrival, the fire department found that the fire involved five vehicles in the ground floor parking area of the flat and it also involved part of the garage with an estimated 50 per cent damage. “No casualties were reported in the incident and the fire department managed to control the fire at 4.38 am before the fire was completely extinguished at 5.09 am,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Zahid sets year-end deadline for legal safeguards on Mara’s RM23b assets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act 1966 will serve as a legal safeguard to ensure the Bumiputera empowerment agenda remains intact and is not altered with every change in national or organisational leadership. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the move is aimed at establishing a stronger governance framework and stricter regulations to ensure the agency’s direction remains consistent in upholding Bumiputera interests, free from political influence. “We do not want Mara’s agenda to change simply because of a change in leadership.

items.title: Anwar: No elitism in Madani’s Bumiputera empowerment approach
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The Bumiputera empowerment agenda has been pursued more assertively, firmly and boldly under the Madani Government over the past three years, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar, who is also finance minister, said the approach demonstrates that the government does not subscribe to elitism at the expense of Bumiputera interests, as alleged by certain quarters. “At the same time, 90 per cent of Bumiputera students continue to be given opportunities through the matriculation programme, reflecting the government’s inclusive approach.

items.title: Search radius grows to 29km for girl swept away in Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
items.summary: KAJANG, May 3 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for a nine-year-old girl who is feared to have drowned after falling into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, entered its third day today with the search being extended to Sungai Langat. Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations commander Izman Adnan said the radius covered a distance of five kilometres (km) from the location of the incident around Pangsapuri Sri Hijau to the end of Sungai Long which connects to Sungai Langat. He said the search today was also carried out from Sungai Langat as far as 24 km towards the Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) water retention pond in Putrajaya, making the total search radius 29 km.

items.title: After controversy in KL, Sarawak rep says Sibu should host ‘Rain Rave’ festivals instead
items.summary: SIBU, May 3 — Bawang Assan assemblyman Datuk Sri Wong Soon Koh has proposed that Sibu host an annual water music festival, saying the district is ready to embrace such initiatives amid controversy surrounding a similar event in Peninsular Malaysia. In a statement, Wong expressed full support for Minister of Tourism, Arts and Culture and Progressive Democratic Party (PDP) president Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing following objections raised against the Rain Rave Water Music Festival in the peninsula. Wong revealed he would formally engage with Tiong to explore the possibility of hosting a similar festival at Sibu Town Square and to propose making it a signature annual event under PDP and Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS), with the aim of positioning Sibu as a tourism and arts hub in central Sarawak.

items.title: Jalan BDC hit-and-run: Kuching mayor appeals for information after cyclist’s death
items.summary: KUCHING, May 3 — The community and netizens are urged to stop speculating on the alleged hit-and-run incident on Friday that killed a 56-year-old cyclist. In making this call, Kuching South mayor Datuk Wee Hong Seng appealed to eye-witnesses to come forward with information to assist the investigation. “While we hope that people could stop speculating and pinning blame, we also urge the culprit to come forward and own up,” he said during his Facebook live session ‘Shall WEE Talk’ on Saturday.

items.title: Father killed, two daughters hurt after trailer crushes family pick-up on LPT2
items.summary: Kemaman police chief Supt Mohd Razi Rosli said the victim, Rosli Yusuf, 55, was pronounced dead at the scene due to severe internal injuries in the incident, which occurred at 6.33 pm. He said the victim’s two daughters, Auni Ukhratan Basyirah, 15, and Iliya Markisa Qurrata Aini, 23, sustained minor injuries and were taken to Kemaman Hospital for treatment. “Initial investigations found that the accident involved a trailer carrying a load of bricks, a Mitsubishi Triton pick-up truck, and a sport utility vehicle (SUV),” he said when contacted today.

items.title: Zahid’s Umno balances solo ambition with alliance flexibility for GE16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is “almost certain” to contest the next general election independently but must remain open to political understandings to avoid disastrous three-cornered fights, said chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. “In the coming election, I am almost certain we will go solo,” Zahid said in the interview for Umno’s 80th anniversary. “We must avoid seat overlaps and three-cornered fights.

items.title: Girl, 10, drowns during family picnic at Jelebu’s Taman Eko Rimba Jeram Toi
items.summary: Jelebu district police chief Supt Azizan Said said his department received information about the incident from a 38-year-old local man who is also a member of the Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM) at 5.44 pm. During the incident, the victim and her mother were believed to have slipped and fell into the pond. “The victim’s mother, however, managed to save herself, while the victim sank to the bottom of the pond.

items.title: George Town hosts grand Vaisakhi open house at Padang Kota Lama
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 3 — The Penang-level Vaisakhi Open House at Jalan Padang Kota Lama drew more than 1,000 visitors from within and outside the state, in a vibrant celebration filled with a spirit of harmony. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the meaningful celebration, now in its 22nd edition in Penang, reflected the enduring presence of the Sikh community in the state as well as a shared commitment to cultural harmony and mutual respect. Chow said Penang’s success has always been driven by its people, who continue to work hand in hand, respect one another and move forward together as one.

items.title: MyDigital ID integration with MyJPJ running smoothly since May 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The implementation of the MyDigital ID Single Sign-On (SSO) integration for the MyJPJ application, enforced since May 1, has been operating smoothly without any technical disruptions. MyDigital ID said in a statement that the system remained stable and was functioning as planned, enabling users to access services more easily and securely. It said that to date, seven million MyJPJ users had registered and logged in using MyDigital ID, with the total number of MyDigital ID users reaching 12 million.

items.title: Search continues for 9-year-old girl swept away in Cheras drain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — The search for a girl, nine, who went missing after falling into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras Friday was suspended at 7.30 pm yesterdat and resumed this morning. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said 22 personnel, including seven water rescue team members were involved in yesterday’s operations. The search involved an area of about five-kilometre radius, he added.

items.title: Palm oil tanker flips, spills contents near Bandar Sultan Suleiman in Klang
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 3 — A palm oil tanker overturned and spilled its content on the road heading to Bandar Sultan Suleiman near here yesterday. North Klang district police chief S. Vijaya Rao said at about 1.55 pm the lorry involved, which was from Rantau Panjang, Klang, was going down a bridge when the tank lock came off the lorry’s head, causing the tank to fall into the left lane of the highway exit. Earlier, a video went viral on social media showing an oil tanker on a road in Klang suddenly loosen from lorry and overturned, spilling oil on the road.

items.title: Penang sees first heat stroke death of 2026 as runner collapses near end of 30km race
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 3 — The Penang State Health Department (JKNPP) has announced that a local man has died from heat stroke, which was reported on April 26, after the victim participated in a 30-kilometre running event in the city. JKNPP said the case involving a 42-year-old man was the first case of heat-related illness recorded in Penang in 2026. “The man participated in a 30-kilometre running event in George Town which started at 6.30 am on April 25 and the running route also involved hilly tracks through forest areas around the city and at about 4 pm, the victim was reported to have fainted near the finish line.

items.title: Viral clip captures moments when bystanders subdue armed woman at Rain Rave Music Festival in KL (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — A 28-year-old woman armed with a knife was arrested on the final night of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival in Bukit Bintang yesterday, with a viral video showing members of the public bravely intervening to subdue her before police arrived. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus confirmed the arrest, which occurred amidst a crowd of approximately 30,000 attendees. “Tonight, we have arrested a 28-year-old woman for possession of a weapon,” Fadil told the media after the event.

items.title: Zahid: Political cooperation no longer optional, but Malay interests remain priority
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today that political cooperation is now essential rather than optional, but stressed that it must be grounded in clear and firm principles. The Federal Constitution must remain the foundation, and the social contract must continue to bind us. “We must approach politics with wisdom and maturity,” he said.

items.title: Khairy: Umno’s image only restored ‘to some extent’, faces uphill battle to regain trust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Khairy Jamaluddin today said Umno must acknowledge its past mistakes as a party before it can begin rebuilding trust with voters who have turned away from it. He said the party’s image had improved only “to some extent”. Khairy said Umno must recognise its past failings collectively, while also noting what he described as the party’s wider contributions to the country.

items.title: Khairy: No official Umno role yet, too early to share details
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Khairy Jamaluddin today said he does not currently hold any official position in Umno. He added that it was still too early to disclose details of any responsibilities he may take on. “I have been asked to look into one or two specific matters, but it is still too early to share details as I have not formally begun,” he told reporters when met at the Umno 80th anniversary convention here today.
